regions.us-ports: Today, Miami, the lead port, is experiencing clear skies and a less than 10% rain probability, with winds from the west-northwest at 4 knots, making for smooth embarkation and disembarkation operations. In the same timeframe, Tampa Bay will see similar conditions, with overcast skies and less than 10% rain probability, and winds from the south at 13 knots, which may affect shore excursions. Beyond 48 hours, Miami and the other primary US cruise homeports can expect continued overcast skies with less than 10% rain probability, and winds from the south-southeast at 11 knots, which should not impact cruise ship operations.
